# ICHRA vs QSEHRA: A Complete 2026 Comparison — Washington Health Insurance Agency

## Summary
2026 comparison of the two employer-funded health reimbursement arrangements (HRAs) that serve as alternatives to traditional group health plans for early-stage startups. ICHRA is the flexible, any-size option with no contribution cap; QSEHRA is the simpler small-employer option capped by IRS annual limits. Both allow tax-free reimbursement of individual health insurance premiums without requiring the employer to offer a group plan.

## Key quotations / statistics
- **ICHRA:** "Available to employers of any size, from startups to large companies. No annual cap on contributions; employers can set different allowance amounts for different employee classes (full-time vs. part-time, by location, etc.)"
- **QSEHRA:** "Limited to employers with fewer than 50 full-time equivalent employees that do not offer traditional group health insurance. Annual IRS-set limits; for 2025, approximately $6,150 for self-only coverage and $12,450 for family coverage. Must be offered on same terms to all eligible full-time employees."
- ICHRA market growth: "2.8x year-over-year growth; 83.5% of 2025 ICHRA adoptees having not previously offered health coverage. A 9 out of 10 renewal rate shows strong employer satisfaction."
- "ICHRA works well for businesses with remote teams, employees in multiple states, or variable-hour workers needing flexibility"
- "QSEHRA is designed as a simpler option for small businesses seeking straightforward health benefits without group plan complexity"
- ACA coverage trigger: Employers with 50+ full-time equivalent employees are required to offer minimum essential coverage (ACA employer mandate)

## Annotations for stinger-forge
- ICHRA is the recommended option for multi-state remote startups at the 1-49 employee range — its class-based allowance structure accommodates geographic salary bands and avoids the QSEHRA same-terms requirement
- QSEHRA's 2025 caps ($6,150 self-only, $12,450 family) are the IRS limits; update if IRS publishes 2026 caps before stinger-forge runs
- The 83.5% ICHRA adoption-without-prior-coverage stat is compelling evidence for bootstrapped startups that ICHRA is a legitimate path to offering "benefits" before they can afford group plans
- `guides/04-benefits-brokerage.md` should include a 4-way comparison: Group plan (ACA), ICHRA, QSEHRA, and PeopleKeep HRA. This source covers ICHRA and QSEHRA well; PeopleKeep will need a separate source
- ACA 50-employee trigger is the key threshold in the benefits decision tree: under 50 FTE → ICHRA/QSEHRA or no-mandate group plan; 50+ FTE → ACA employer mandate applies
